name: Henry Coker
abstract: Henry Coker (December 24, 1919 – November 23, 1979) was an American jazz trombonist.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert featuring Count Basie and His Orchestra, Charlie Parker, and Sarah Vaughan

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: In 1951 he joined the Count Basie Orchestra and quickly began sharing the trombone solo chores with section mate Henry Coker.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: In 1951 he joined the Count Basie Orchestra and quickly began sharing the trombone solo chores with section mate Henry Coker.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: we had a good little band with Eddie Hayward and Vic Dickenson. Henry Coker (PHONETIC) played trombones.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Trombones were Henry Coker, Benny Powell, and the composer-arranger Johnny Mandel... and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...Trumpets; Wendell Cully, Snooky Young, Thad Jones, Joe Newman... Trombones; Henry Coker... And we‘re going to pick up with the tail end of the Count Basie time, that you‘re with Count Basie the second time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Al Grey
about_quotes.evidence: And here’s Sonny Payne and Frank Wess and me and later I just -- and Henry Coker who was a trombone player, and Benny Powell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Grey interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 3, 1995
